name: Albuquerque Corn-Bread Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
Prepare corn bread: Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Grease a 13x9 metal baking pan or deep 12-inch skillet with an oven-safe handle.
In a large bowl, mix the cornmeal, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Stir in the buttermilk and remaining ingredients except chicken broth until thoroughly blended. Pour batter into the baking pan.
Bake corn bread for 60 to 65 minutes or until the top is browned and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool corn bread on a wire rack (if not making stuffing right away, cover and reserve up to 2 days).
Prepare stuffing: Crumble the cooled corn bread into a large bowl. Drizzle with chicken broth; toss to mix well. Use to stuff a 12 to 16 lb turkey or spoon into a 13x9 glass baking dish, cover with foil, and bake in a preheated 325-degree oven for 45 minutes or until heated through.
